description: American Protestant theologian (1892–1971)
description_wiki: Karl Paul Reinhold Niebuhr (June 21, 1892 – June 1, 1971) was an American Reformed theologian, ethicist, commentator on politics and public affairs, and professor at Union Theological Seminary for more than 30 years.

description_wiki: Amherst County is a county, located in the Piedmont region and near the center of the Commonwealth of Virginia in the United States.

description_wiki: Sweet Briar College (Sweet Briar, SBC) is a private women's liberal arts college in Sweet Briar, Amherst County, Virginia, United States.

description: most populous city in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
description_wiki: Cologne (  kə-LOHN; German: Köln [kœln] ; Kölsch: Kölle [ˈkœ̂lə] ) is the fourth-most populous city of Germany and the largest city of the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia with nearly 1.1 million inhabitants in the city proper and over 3.1 million people in the Cologne Bonn urban region.

description: English real estate entrepreneur, philosopher, early Quaker and founder of the Province of Pennsylvania (1644-1718)
description_wiki: William Penn (24 October [O.S. 14 October] 1644 – 10 August [O.S. 30 July] 1718) was an English writer, theologian, religious thinker, and influential Quaker who founded the Province of Pennsylvania.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Pennsylvania, officially the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, is a U.S. state located in the Mid-Atlantic, Northeastern, Appalachian, and Great Lakes regions of the United States.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: American white supremacist hate group
description_wiki: The Ku Klux Klan (KKK; ), sometimes referred to as the Klan, is an American Protestant-led white supremacist and far-right hate group.

description: borough of Pennsylvania

description_wiki: The University of Lynchburg, formerly Lynchburg College, is a private university associated with the Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) and located in Lynchburg, Virginia, United States.

World's Alliance of Young Men's Christian Associations
description: worldwide organization
description_wiki: YMCA, sometimes regionally called the Y, is a worldwide youth organization based in Vernier, Canton of Geneva, Switzerland, with more than 64 million beneficiaries in 120 countries.

description: federation of Student Christian Movements
description_wiki: The World Student Christian Federation (WSCF) is a federation of autonomous national Christian student organizations forming the youth and student arm of the global ecumenical movement.

description: commune in Meuse, France
description_wiki: Verdun ( vur-DUN, UK also  VAIR-dun, US also  vair-DUN; French: [vɛʁdœ̃] ; official name before 1970: Verdun-sur-Meuse) is a city in the Meuse department in Grand Est, northeastern France.

description: president of the United States from 1913 to 1921 (1856–1924)
description_wiki: Thomas Woodrow Wilson (December 28, 1856 – February 3, 1924) was the 28th president of the United States, serving from 1913 to 1921.

description: British sprinter
description_wiki: William Craner (17 July 1906 – 12 January 1987) was a British sprinter.

description: private university in Nashville, Tennessee, US
description_wiki: Vanderbilt University (informally Vandy or VU) is a private research university in Nashville, Tennessee, United States.

description: Australian politician
description_wiki: Dean Craig Brown, AO (born 5 April 1943) is a politician who served as the Premier of South Australia between 14 December 1993 and 28 November 1996, and also served as 10th Deputy Premier of South Australia between 22 October 2001 and 5 March 2002, representing the South Australian Division of the Liberal Party of Australia.
